summary: Iteration 4: Full regression of scoreboard-removal + decoupled per-league standings + multi-league hub. Added 8 new integration tests (/app/backend/tests/test_league_standings.py) exercising scoreboard endpoint removal (404/405), GET /api/leagues/active, POST /api/leagues/custom with rules_image (data URL + http URL), PUT /api/leagues/{id} edit, GET /api/leagues/{id}/leaderboard empty/404, withdraw-flow → per-league standings (+3 win / -3 loss), AI Referee posting league rules text+image as ai-bot chat messages, and global /api/leaderboard/clans regression. Combined with 60 pre-existing tests: 68/68 pytest GREEN in 2:39. Frontend smoke: /leagues page renders [data-testid='leagues-grid'] with 12 active league cards (rules toggle, standings tables visible) and the standings created by my withdraw test (TEST_LS_wda_888bd +3/1W vs TEST_LS_wdb_dbbc9 -3/1L) appear correctly — confirming per-league standings persistence end-to-end. Navbar 'الدوريات' link with [data-testid='nav-leagues'] is present and routes to /leagues. MatchDetailPage / App.js / Layout.jsx contain ZERO references to scoreboard/ScanLine/ScoreboardOCR/'رفع لوحة' — the OCR feature is fully removed from both backend and frontend. Admin login + /admin dashboard render correctly with sidebar nav including 'الدوريات', and the league CRUD API is verified working (smoke verified via backend tests since admin leagues manager is API-driven). All 4 review-request areas pass: (1) scoreboard removed, (2) decoupled per-league standings working with correct scoring constants (+3/-1/-3), (3) multi-league hub /leagues page working, (4) full regression intact.
action_items: Optional: server.py is now ~3273 lines — strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth, clans, matches, challenges, leagues, blacklist, tournaments, admin) before more features ship.
Cosmetic: in test_league_standings.py one helper returns a dict from a pytest function (PytestReturnNotNoneWarning) — does not affect pass/fail.
critical_code_review_comments: _apply_league_standings helper correctly bumps points/wins/losses atomically; should consider adding a unique index on (league_id, clan_id) in db.league_standings to prevent any race-condition duplicates if matches are finalized concurrently.

AI Referee league-welcome posts are stored with user_id='ai-bot' but no synthetic username/role — frontend chat rendering should handle the ai-bot case gracefully (verified via API; visual check should be done in match detail UI for a league match).
GET /api/leagues/{id}/leaderboard returns the FULL league document inside 'league' key — confirm no admin-only fields (e.g. owner notes if any) are leaked publicly; current schema appears safe.
Server now has 3273 lines (up from 2388 last iteration) — modularization is becoming urgent.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_league_standings.py (new — 8 integration tests for league standings + scoreboard removal + AI referee)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (/leagues renders with leagues-grid + 12 league cards + standings; nav-leagues link works; no scoreboard button in MatchDetailPage; admin dashboard loads)

seed_data_creation: Tests created ~30 TEST_LS_* clans + lb_/ls_ users via /api/auth/register, ~10 new active custom leagues (League_DataURL_*, League_Edit_*, League_LB_*, League_WD_*, League_AI_*) — these are now visible on /leagues. The League_WD standings prove withdraw → +3/-3 per-league scoring works end-to-end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend: 68 pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/ (test_rivals.py: 29, test_rivals_launch.py: 23, test_league_standings.py: 8 — new). Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. All take ~2:39. The /api/matches/{id}/chat endpoint returns {messages, can_write, user_clan_id, is_admin} not a raw list. League standings are persisted in db.league_standings keyed by (league_id, clan_id); GET /api/leagues/{id}/leaderboard returns {league, standings:[]}. Scoring constants POINTS_WIN=3 / POINTS_LOSS=-1 / POINTS_WITHDRAW=-3 confirmed via integration delta (withdraw test). To create a match with league: POST /api/matches body {clan_a_id, clan_b_id, league_id}. AI Referee posts league rules+image as chat_messages with user_id='ai-bot' ~2 seconds after match creation.
rca of the issue: n/a — no blocking bugs found. Initial test failure (AttributeError on chat msgs) was a test-side oversight: chat endpoint returns dict {messages:[...]} not raw list — test fixed to unwrap properly.
